title: Dr. Rezwana Karim Snigdha — Feminist Anthropologist, Bangladesh
description: Dr. Rezwana Karim Snigdha is one of Bangladesh's foremost feminist anthropologists — Associate Professor at Jahangirnagar University, known for ethnographic research on Hijra communities, gender, body and belonging in South Asia.
heroRole: Associate Professor of Anthropology, Jahangirnagar University · PhD, Auckland University of Technology
grant: World Bank / UGC HEAT Grant — USD 80,000 · 2025 · Top 10.2% nationally
aboutQuote: Ethnographic explorations at the threshold of gender, body, and belonging.
about: Dr. Rezwana Karim Snigdha is an anthropologist, gender expert, and feminist scholar with over fifteen years of experience in teaching, ethnographic research, training, and advocacy at Jahangirnagar University, where she serves as Associate Professor of Anthropology.

She earned her PhD at the Auckland University of Technology (2021); her doctoral thesis, “Beyond Binaries: An Ethnographic Study of Hijra in Dhaka, Bangladesh,” is recognised internationally for its sensitive, situated account of gender-diverse life in South Asia.

Her work moves across gender justice, sexuality and reproductive health, body politics, climate vulnerability, and medical anthropology — always centred on marginalised and gender-diverse communities. She is a member of the Sex Tech Lab at The New School, New York, and holds part-time faculty and review roles at home and abroad.

gallery.caption: Keynote · Dept. of Anthropology, University of Rajshahi
Panel · public discussion on gender & rights

address: Department of Anthropology, Jahangirnagar University, Savar, Dhaka-1342, Bangladesh
